title: Differentiate the acute presentation
prompt: What findings in Hailey's presentation make acute asthma more likely than pneumonia or pneumothorax?
hint: Use the combination of wheeze, hyperinflation, eosinophilia, and absent infiltrates.
answer: The child has wheeze, nocturnal symptoms, mucus production, hyperinflation on chest x-ray, eosinophilia, and no infiltrate or pneumothorax, which strongly supports acute asthma exacerbation.

title: Pause before stepping up therapy
prompt: Why is adherence and inhaler technique the first management step in the primary-care asthma case?
answer: A patient can look uncontrolled because of poor adherence or poor inhaler use, so escalating medication before checking these issues can be the wrong next step.

title: Monitor the rescue therapy
prompt: Which laboratory and cardiac findings should make you suspect beta2-agonist induced hypokalemia after repeated albuterol treatment?
hint: Tie the adverse effect to intracellular potassium shift and ECG change.
answer: Potassium 2.6 mEq/L with weakness, nausea, and U waves on ECG after repeated albuterol treatments strongly suggests beta2-agonist induced intracellular potassium shift.

title: Anchor the histology
prompt: What two histologic findings from the cardio lesson should you name when identifying cardiac muscle?
hint: Think nuclei location and junctional structures.
answer: Cardiac muscle in this module is identified by centrally placed nuclei and intercalated discs (with striations also present).

title: Mechanism chain
prompt: State the beta1 signaling chain from norepinephrine to increased cardiac work, then describe what metoprolol blocks.
hint: Use receptor -> Gs -> cAMP -> calcium -> contractility/heart rate.
answer: Norepinephrine activates beta1 receptors, increasing Gs signaling, cAMP, and calcium-dependent contractility and rate. Metoprolol competitively blocks beta1 receptors, reducing this cascade and lowering cardiac work.

title: Follow-up targets
prompt: Which two objective measurements should improve first if metoprolol is working in this case?
hint: Use one vital sign and one pressure metric.
answer: An early response should show a lower resting heart rate and an improving blood pressure trend on serial checks.
